description: Harden reference-identity projection compilation so `DocumentReferenceBinding.FkColumn` is validated as the actual stored reference-site presence column. Acceptance: read-plan compilation fails deterministically when a binding FK column is missing, is not `ColumnKind.DocumentFk`, or is not backed by `ColumnStorage.Stored`; successful compilation only allows the stored `..._DocumentId` column to drive `FkColumnOrdinal`, which preserves the design invariant that reference-object presence is determined by the real FK column rather than a scalar or unified alias.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests that mutate a `DocumentReferenceBinding` to point at a scalar column, a unified alias column, and a missing column, and assert `ReadPlanCompiler` fails with actionable messages for each case.
Retain or add a positive test proving a valid stored `DocumentFk` column still compiles to the expected `FkColumnOrdinal`.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler`

description: Validate that compiled reference and descriptor projection bindings still target the API-bound columns for their declared JSON paths. Acceptance: for every `DocumentReferenceBinding`, the FK column resolves to `SourceJsonPath == ReferenceObjectPath` and each `ReferenceIdentityBinding.Column` resolves to `SourceJsonPath == ReferenceJsonPath`; for every `DescriptorEdgeSource`, the binding/path column resolves to `SourceJsonPath == DescriptorValuePath`; compilation fails deterministically if a storage-only canonical column, unrelated alias, or mismatched path column is wired into projection metadata, especially in key-unification scenarios where path columns and storage columns differ.
steps-to-verify: Add negative unit tests for mismatched `SourceJsonPath` on reference FK columns, reference identity columns, and descriptor binding columns, including at least one key-unified model that incorrectly points at a canonical storage column.
Add a positive key-unification test proving projection compilation still accepts valid per-path unified aliases while descriptor SQL deduplicates by storage column.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler`

description: Consolidate projection-contract validation into one authoritative implementation shared by runtime compilation and normalized DTO decode. Acceptance: compile-time and decode-time validation enforce the same rules for projection table membership, binding/source counts, ordinals, result shapes, path/resource identity, and `IsIdentityComponent`; duplicated validation logic is removed or reduced to thin adapters; malformed projection metadata fails deterministically at the chosen validation boundary instead of relying on partially overlapping checks in multiple places.
steps-to-verify: Refactor the current projection validation flow so one shared validator is used by both `ReadPlanCompiler` and normalized read-plan decode paths.
Add or update tests proving the same malformed projection payload fails identically whether it is compiled directly or reconstructed through `NormalizedPlanContractCodec`.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler|FullyQualifiedName~Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Harden read-plan projection validation so reference-identity projection plans must cover each modeled `DocumentReferenceBinding` exactly once instead of only matching the total binding count. Acceptance: `ReadPlanProjectionContractValidator` rejects a plan when one compiled binding is duplicated, when one modeled binding is omitted, or when projection bindings appear in an order that does not match the authoritative `DocumentReferenceBindings` order for the owning table; valid plans compiled by `ReadPlanCompiler` still pass without changing the expected ordinals or SQL.
steps-to-verify: Add negative tests that construct a `ResourceReadPlan` or decoded DTO with the same reference projection binding repeated twice while another modeled binding is missing, and assert validation fails with an actionable message that identifies the missing or duplicate binding.
Add a negative test that swaps two modeled reference projection bindings for the same table and assert validation fails because the authoritative projection order was not preserved.
Retain or add a positive test proving a valid compiled plan for a resource with multiple reference bindings still passes validation unchanged.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler|FullyQualifiedName~Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Harden read-plan projection validation so descriptor projection plans must cover each modeled `DescriptorEdgeSource` exactly once instead of only matching the total source count. Acceptance: `ReadPlanProjectionContractValidator` rejects a plan when one descriptor source is duplicated, when one modeled descriptor source is omitted, or when `SourcesInOrder` no longer preserves the deterministic execution order required by the model; valid descriptor projection plans, including key-unified storage deduplication scenarios, still pass with the current canonical SQL.
steps-to-verify: Add negative tests that construct a `ResourceReadPlan` or decoded DTO with the same descriptor source repeated twice while another modeled source is missing, and assert validation fails with an actionable message that identifies the missing or duplicate source.
Add a negative test that reverses two descriptor projection sources and assert validation fails because the authoritative descriptor source order was not preserved.
Retain or add a positive key-unification test proving logical descriptor sources remain distinct while SQL input deduplicates by storage column.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler|FullyQualifiedName~Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Restore deterministic projection-order guarantees in normalized read-plan decode so AOT and manifest consumers cannot accept reordered projection metadata that still happens to be individually valid. Acceptance: `NormalizedPlanContractCodec.Decode` and the shared projection validator reject decoded read plans whose `ReferenceIdentityProjectionPlansInDependencyOrder`, per-table `BindingsInOrder`, `DescriptorProjectionPlansInOrder`, or per-plan `SourcesInOrder` differ from the authoritative model order; round-trip encode/decode remains stable for valid plans and continues to preserve the exact projection ordering required by the design documents.
steps-to-verify: Add decode-path negative tests that reorder reference projection table plans, per-table reference bindings, descriptor projection plans, and descriptor sources, and assert `NormalizedPlanContractCodec.Decode` fails with deterministic validation messages.
Add or update round-trip tests proving a valid encoded read plan still re-encodes to the same canonical JSON and preserves projection collection ordering byte-for-byte.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec`

description: Remove the descriptor projection compiler's incidental dependency on hydration select-list ordinals for storage-only canonical descriptor columns. Acceptance: descriptor projection SQL ordering is derived from authoritative table-model column order rather than `columnOrdinalsByTable`; compilation continues to deduplicate descriptor SQL by `(table, storage column)` for key-unified models; valid models compile even if a future hydration plan no longer selects storage-only canonical descriptor columns, while invalid descriptor bindings still fail deterministically with actionable messages.
steps-to-verify: Refactor `DescriptorProjectionPlanCompiler` so the ordering for deduplicated SQL sources comes from `DbTableModel.Columns` or equivalent table-model metadata instead of hydration projection ordinals for the storage column.

Add a regression test covering a key-unified descriptor model where the binding/path columns remain hydrated but the canonical storage column is treated as storage-only for descriptor SQL ordering, and assert compilation still succeeds with deterministic SQL and source ordering.
Retain or add negative tests proving missing tables, missing binding columns, or non-`DescriptorFk` bindings still fail with deterministic compiler errors.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler`

description: Tighten descriptor projection contract validation so each compiled `DescriptorProjectionPlan` is structurally valid on its own instead of only participating in a flattened source-count check. Acceptance: validation rejects any read plan that contains an empty descriptor projection plan, a plan whose sources do not form a valid contiguous slice of the authoritative `DescriptorEdgeSources`, or extra descriptor result sets that runtime would otherwise execute without consuming useful sources; valid split-plan and single-plan descriptor projections continue to round-trip through normalized DTO decode and compile successfully.
steps-to-verify: Update `ReadPlanProjectionContractValidator` so each descriptor projection plan must contain at least one source and preserve deterministic source coverage without allowing trailing empty plans or other structurally empty result sets.

Add negative tests for `[plan-with-all-sources, empty-plan]`, `[]` sources within a non-empty descriptor plan, and any equivalent malformed DTO decode case, and assert validation fails with deterministic messages.
Retain or add positive tests proving both the compiler-generated single-plan case and an intentional multi-plan split case still validate successfully.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler|FullyQualifiedName~Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`

description: Eliminate duplicated projection-plan mutation helpers across the plans unit-test suites so projection-contract tests are maintained from one shared set of utilities. Acceptance: helper methods for duplicating, swapping, and mutating reference-identity and descriptor projection plans are defined once in a shared test utility or base type; `Given_ReadPlanCompiler` and `Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec` reuse the shared helpers without changing existing test intent; the refactor reduces copy-pasted helper code while keeping all current projection validation scenarios intact.
steps-to-verify: Extract the duplicated read-plan mutation helpers from `ReadPlanCompilerTests.cs` and `NormalizedPlanContractCodecTests.cs` into a shared test helper or test base and update both suites to use it.

Confirm the refactor preserves the existing projection validation assertions for duplicated bindings, reordered bindings, duplicated descriptor sources, reordered descriptor sources, and reordered descriptor plans.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Given_ReadPlanCompiler|FullyQualifiedName~Given_NormalizedPlanContractCodec"`
